title: The Siddur (Prayer Book): Your Guide to Daily Jewish Prayer
title_zh: 祈祷书(Siddur):犹太人日常祈祷指南
author: Torah Light Team
target_audience: Mandarin-speaking beginners with no prior Jewish knowledge
description: A comprehensive guide to the Jewish prayer book (siddur), its history, structure, and practical use. Explores morning, afternoon, and evening prayers, different traditions (Ashkenaz, Sefard, Ari), and how to choose and use a siddur with cultural parallels to Chinese classical texts.
description_zh: 犹太祈祷书(Siddur)的综合指南,包括其历史、结构和实际用途。探讨晨祷、午祷和晚祷,不同传统(阿什肯纳兹、塞法迪、阿里),以及如何选择和使用祈祷书,并与中国经典文本进行文化比较。
description_he: מדריך מקיף לסידור התפילה היהודי, ההיסטוריה, המבנה והשימוש המעשי שלו. בוחן תפילות שחרית, מנחה וערבית, מסורות שונות (אשכנז, ספרד, אר"י), וכיצד לבחור ולהשתמש בסידור עם הקבלות תרבותיות לטקסטים קלאסיים סיניים.
chinese_parallels: Analects (论语) as classical moral texts
Spring and Autumn Period (春秋时代, 770-476 BCE)
Confucian concept of Ren (仁) - community relationships
Respect for Four Books (四书) and Five Classics (五经)
key_concepts: Nusach Ashkenaz, Nusach Sefard, Nusach Ari
practical_guidance: Learning one section at a time
siddur_editions_mentioned: Seder Rav Amram Gaon (first written siddur)

sources.title: Jewish Liturgy: A Comprehensive History
sources.author: Ismar Elbogen
sources.description: Comprehensive history of the development of Jewish prayer

sources.title: To Pray as a Jew: A Guide to the Prayer Book and the Synagogue Service
sources.author: Rabbi Hayim Halevy Donin
sources.description: Classic introductory guide to Jewish prayer

sources.title: My People's Prayer Book series
sources.description: Multi-volume commentary on the siddur with diverse perspectives

sources.title: Sefaria.org - Liturgy Texts
sources.description: Siddur Ashkenaz and Sefard editions with English translation

sources.title: Chabad.org - The Jewish Prayer Book
sources.description: Introduction to the siddur and Jewish prayer

sources.title: Aish.com - How to Pray: A Beginner's Guide
sources.description: Practical beginner's guide to Jewish prayer

cultural_sensitivity_notes: Draws parallels to Chinese classical texts (Analects, Four Books, Five Classics) to help Mandarin speakers understand the role of liturgy
References Chinese dynasties parallel to Jewish historical periods
Compares Confucian concept of Ren (仁) to Jewish community-focused spirituality
Addresses practical challenges for Chinese Jews (limited Mandarin resources)
Mentions Chabad centers in Shanghai, Beijing, and Hong Kong as resources
pedagogical_approach: Starts with simple definition and purpose
Provides historical context with Chinese parallels
Breaks down complex structure into digestible sections
Offers practical guidance for choosing and using a siddur
Balances spiritual meaning with practical instruction
Encourages gradual learning rather than overwhelming detail
Emphasizes both form (structure) and soul (personal connection)

quality_check.chinese_parallels: ✓ Multiple historical and philosophical parallels included
quality_check.bibliography: ✓ Complete with primary texts, secondary sources, and online resources
quality_check.practical_guidance: ✓ Concrete advice for choosing and using a siddur
quality_check.beginner_friendly: ✓ Clear explanations without assuming prior knowledge
